  r8_go/src/utils/structural/core.go to see if
  there's an interface I can leverage.

╭─────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ ✓  ReadFile r8_go/src/utils/structural/cor… │
│                                             │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────╯
✦ DexType, DexField, DexMethod, and DexProto have
  CompareTo methods, but they don't exactly match
  the Ordered or StructuralItem interfaces in
  utils/structural. I'll now search for a
  DexReference interface in the dex package to see
  if it exists and how it's defined.

╭─────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ ✓  Shell grep -r "type .* interface" . [in… │
│                                             │
│ Command exited with code: 1                 │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────╯
✦ No interfaces in the dex package? That's
  surprising. I'll check binary_reader.go and
  vdex.go to see if I missed anything there.

╭─────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ ✓  ReadFile r8_go/src/dex/binary_reader.go  │
│                                             │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────╯
✦ Formalizing DexItem and DexReference as Go
  interfaces hasn't happened yet, as porting is
  still ongoing. The current manual type
  dispatching isn't ideal for Go, but it's where we
  are. I'll check r8_go/src/dex/dex_edge_test.go to
  see if there are any other interesting
  developments.

╭─────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ ✓  ReadFile r8_go/src/dex/dex_edge_test.go  │
│                                             │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────╯
✦ I've made significant headway today by
  refactoring dex_string.go into specialized files,
  expanding DexItemFactory with more common Java
  types, and centralizing the Sign utility. Now,
  I'll take a look at dex_value.go to see if
  there's anything else in the dex package that
  needs attention.

╭─────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ ✓  ReadFile r8_go/src/dex/dex_value.go      │
│                                             │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────╯
✦ DexValue looks like a barebones placeholder right
  now, especially compared to the full hierarchy in
  R8. I'll check encoded_value.go to see if the
  more complex EncodedValue types are already
  implemented.

╭─────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ ✓  ReadFile r8_go/src/dex/encoded_value.go  │
│                                             │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────╯
✦ encoded_value.go is just encoding helpers, so the
  full DexValue hierarchy from R8—including types
  like DexValueInt, DexValueString, and
  DexValueArray—is missing. Since I've already
  tackled the other basic Dex graph nodes, DexValue
  is the logical next step. I'll check
  dex_value_test.go to see what's already covered
  before I start implementing the hierarchy.

╭─────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ ✓  ReadFile r8_go/src/dex/encoded_value_te… │
│                                             │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────╯
✦ encoded_value_test.go only covers encoding, and
  while encoded_value.go uses math/bits, my test
  could use it too. I'm moving on to DexValue,
  which in R8 is a full-fledged DexItem subclass.
  I'll define it as an interface in Go, but first,
  I'll check for an existing DexValueVisitor to see
  if I need to implement one.

╭─────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ ✓  Shell grep -r "DexValueVisitor" . [in r… │
│                                             │
│ Command exited with code: 1                 │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────╯
✦ No visitor found. I'll move on to implementing
  DexValueByte, DexValueInt, and DexValueString, as
  DexItem is currently missing and likely just a
  marker interface anyway. I've already refactored
  dex_string.go, expanded DexItemFactory, and
  extracted Sign, so I'll take a look at header.go
  and map_entries.go next to see if they need
  similar treatment.

╭─────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ ✓  ReadFile r8_go/src/dex/header.go         │
│                                             │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────╯
✦ header.go is solid, so I'll check
  r8_go/src/dex/container next.

╭─────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ ✓  ReadFolder r8_go/src/dex/container       │
│                                             │
│ Listed 2 item(s).                           │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────╯
✦ Checking r8_go/src/dex/container/policy.go.

╭─────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ ✓  ReadFile r8_go/src/dex/container/policy… │
│                                             │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────╯
✦ I've improved the dex package's structure and
  testing, and now I'm considering DexValue as the
  next step. But first, I'll check
  base_compiler_command_parser.go and
  parse_flag_info_impl.go to see if they need
  refactoring.

╭─────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ ✓  ReadFile .../base_compiler_command_pars… │
│                                             │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────╯
✦ Code looks good. I've refactored dex_string.go,
  expanded DexItemFactory, moved the Sign utility,
  and updated the tests. I'm ready to move on to
  DexValue or whatever's next on the agenda. Any
  preference?
